title: Ensure the default security group of every VPC restricts all  traffic

input_row.title: Ensure the default security group of every VPC restricts all  traffic
input_row.description: VPC security groups provide stateful filtering of ingress/egress network traffic to Virtual Server. It is recommended that no security group allows unrestricted ingress access to a Virtual Server. Unless modified, the default security group allows inbound traffic from all members of the group that is, all other virtual servers that are attached to this security group.
input_row.rationale: Removing uncontrolled connectivity to a Virtual Server, reduces a server's exposure to risk.
input_row.audit: Perform the following tasks to determine if the account is configured as prescribed: 1. Login to the IBM Cloud Portal at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. At the Menu icon, select VPC Infrastructure-->VPC Layout and Security Groups. 3. For the default security group, perform the following: a. Ensure no Inbound Rule exists that allows unrequited traffic to the Virtual Servers.
input_row.remediation: 1. Login to the IBM Cloud Portal at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. At the Menu icon, select VPC Infrastructure-->VPC Layout and Security Groups. 3. For the default security group, perform the following: a. Identify the Inbound rule. b. Update the rule to only allow the required traffic flow.
input_row.impact: For updating an existing environment, care should be taken to ensure that Virtual Servers currently relying on ingress have the required access.
input_row.default_value: Unless modified, the default security group allows inbound traffic from all members of the group that is, all other virtual servers that are attached to this security group.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control regarding the default security group for VPCs allows for API-based verification since the relevant properties can be queried via IBM Cloud APIs and the IBM Cloud CLI. The audit and remediation processes provided clear steps for checking the inbound rules of the default security group, which indicates that it can be verified programmatically.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_vpc_security_group_default_rules_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **API Calls/CLI Commands:** Use IBM Cloud CLI commands such as `ibmcloud resource service-instance list` and `ibmcloud is security-groups` to enumerate VPC security groups, and subsequently check the inbound rules for each security group using the API method `GetSecurityGroup()` or equivalent CLI commands.
  
- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:** The automation would enumerate security groups across all VPCs within the account. This includes default security groups provided by IBM Cloud.

- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:** Inspect the inbound rules of the default security group. Check that there are no rules allowing unrestricted inbound traffic (i.e., rules that allow traffic from "0.0.0.0/0" or similar configurations).

- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** The criteria for the audit would be:
  - **Pass:** No inbound rules exist that allow unrestricted traffic (i.e., all ingress rules are appropriately restricted). 
  - **Fail:** Any inbound rule exists that allows unrestricted traffic (e.g., allowing all traffic from any source).

This automated approach will ensure that all VPCs in the IBM Cloud account comply with the security standard of restricting all traffic within the default security groups.
